step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the selection of polycarboxylate cement as it offers good mechanical properties close to those of natural tooth structure, ensuring better integration and longevity of the restoration. Additionally, its flexibility can help accommodate micro-movements without causing stress fractures typical in rigid systems like zinc oxide eugenol (ZOE). Therefore, my final answer remains **C**: Polycarboxylate.
